# UC-16.1.29 · Control-M Job Late Start Beyond Forecast Window

## Description

Late starts compress downstream runtimes and breach business deadlines.

## Value

Quantifies how far jobs slip past forecasted start for capacity planning.

## Implementation

Populate `forecast_window_sec` from job attributes; default 300s if null.

## Detailed Implementation

Prerequisites
• Install and configure: Control-M workload definitions + run metadata via Automation API.
• Data: `controlm:job` events with `scheduled_time`, `start_time`, `forecast_window_sec` (normalized from order/forecast API).
• For directory layout and inputs, see docs/implementation-guide.md.

Step 1 — Configure data collection
Populate `forecast_window_sec` from job attributes; default 300s if null.

Step 2 — Create the search and alert
Run the following search (then save as report or alert; tune time range and threshold):

```spl
index=cicd sourcetype="controlm:job"
| eval late_start=if(isnotnull(scheduled_time) AND isnotnull(start_time) AND start_time>scheduled_time+forecast_window_sec,1,0)
| where late_start=1 AND status IN ("Running","Wait Host","Wait Resource")
| eval delay_min=round((start_time-scheduled_time)/60,1)
| table job_name, folder, scheduled_time, start_time, delay_min, controlm_server
| sort -delay_min
```

Understanding this search
Late starts compress downstream runtimes and breach business deadlines.

Step 3 — Validate
Compare with the Control-M job plan and run history in the job scheduler. Reconcile job and folder names, start and end times, and queue or pool use for a few highlighted jobs.

Step 4 — Operationalize
Add to a dashboard, wire alert actions, and document ownership. Suggested view: Column chart (delay distribution), Table (worst jobs).

## SPL

```spl
index=cicd sourcetype="controlm:job"
| eval late_start=if(isnotnull(scheduled_time) AND isnotnull(start_time) AND start_time>scheduled_time+forecast_window_sec,1,0)
| where late_start=1 AND status IN ("Running","Wait Host","Wait Resource")
| eval delay_min=round((start_time-scheduled_time)/60,1)
| table job_name, folder, scheduled_time, start_time, delay_min, controlm_server
| sort -delay_min
```

## Visualization

Column chart (delay distribution), Table (worst jobs).
